title: Patriots might have shot at top free-agent receiver after Colts’ franchise-tag gamble
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Colts wideout Alec Pierce may enter free agency after Indianapolis applied a transition tag on QB Daniel Jones.
Pierce, 25, led the NFL in yards per catch over the last two seasons, making him a top free-agent target.
Patriots may pursue Pierce to boost their receiver corps, but his price could range from $25-30 million annually.

title: Vermont postal worker allegedly threw away mail she was supposed to deliver for months
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Vermont postal worker Natasha Morisseau, 34, was cited for allegedly discarding mail over several months.
Morisseau faces nine counts of petty larceny and five counts of unlawful mischief, police reported.
USPS suspended Morisseau, and recovered mail was returned for delivery; investigation continues by USPS Inspector General.

title: Man in his 80s, dog dead in Foxborough fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An elderly man and a dog died in a house fire on Granite Street, Foxborough early Tuesday morning.
Firefighters from Foxborough, Mansfield, and Plainville extinguished the fire within an hour, which caused major damage.
The cause of the fire is under investigation by local fire and law enforcement officials.

title: Patriots players brought strippers, hookahs to celebrate at a Boston restaurant, its owner says. He’s now facing a license violation.
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Estella's in Boston risks liquor license for hosting Patriots players with hookahs, strippers, and after-hours drinking.
Owner Helder George Brandao blames players for party getting out of control; police found hookahs, nude women, and cash.
Boston Licensing Board to decide on license penalties, including potential suspension or revocation, for hosting unruly party.

title: Mass. students place No. 1 nationwide for AP scores, break all-time record
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Massachusetts students achieved the highest national scores on AP exams, with 35.8% scoring 3 or higher.
The state set a record for the highest AP exam passing rates, with significant participation from Black or African American students.
Massachusetts subsidized over 90,783 AP exams, aiding nearly 50,000 low-income students, under Gov. Healey's initiative.

title: ‘Forced to take this pay raise’: Quincy City Councilors are collecting the raise they promised to repeal
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Quincy City Council members are receiving a $15,000 raise they had campaigned to repeal before taking office.
New councilors are unable to immediately change raises due to state laws and are opting to donate portions to nonprofits.
The previous council approved 50% raises in June without public hearings, which contributed to five incumbents losing reelection.

title: Rosenthal: Red Sox remain the ‘best fit’ as a trade suitor for Isaac Paredes
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox could trade Jarren Duran to acquire Isaac Paredes from the Astros for infield depth.
Paredes, 27, hit 20 home runs over 102 games for Houston, and Fenway Park might boost his power.
Despite potential benefits, the Red Sox and Astros haven't been in recent contact about a trade.

title: New ESPN mock draft has Patriots choosing a ‘pass-rushing menace’ in first round
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: ESPN mock draft projects Patriots to select Cashius Howell, a pass-rusher from Texas A&M, at No. 31 overall.
Howell recorded 11.5 sacks last season and led the SEC with 41 total pressures for the Aggies.
Patriots face losing K'Lavon Chaisson to free agency; Howell's disruptive style fits New England's defensive needs.

title: Salem State student’s killer convicted 40 years after her murder, prosecutors say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: John Carey, 66, convicted of murdering Salem State student Claire Gravel in 1986, Essex County Superior Court announced.
Carey was identified as the suspect through DNA found on Gravel's clothing and indicted in 2022.
Carey was already serving a sentence for attempted murder at the time of his indictment.

title: N.H. woman accused of civil rights violation after allegedly shooting at lost man because he was Black
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Diane Durgin, 67, charged with shooting at a Black man in Weare, N.H., after he mistakenly arrived at her property.
Durgin accused of violating New Hampshire's Civil Rights Act and faces charges including criminal threatening and attempted first degree assault.
Prosecutors seek injunction to prevent Durgin from contacting victim; she could face fines up to $5,000 per civil rights violation.
